inputs.name: ffData
inputs.description: Flat file data to be converted. The data can be a String, byte array, InputStream, or java.io.Reader object.

inputs.name: ffSchema
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the flat file schema that describes the structure of the flat file data in ffData.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Character encoding to use when reading the flat file data. If not specified, the encoding specified in the flat file schema is used. If no encoding is specified in the schema, the default system encoding is used.

inputs.name: keepResults
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether to return all parsed records or only the last record. Set to: true to return all parsed records in an IData array. false to return only the last parsed record. This is the default.

inputs.name: validate
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether to validate the flat file data against the flat file schema. Set to: true to validate the data. false to skip validation. This is the default.

outputs.name: ffValues
outputs.description: IData object containing the parsed flat file data. The structure of this document corresponds to the structure defined in the flat file schema.

inputs.name: ffValues
inputs.description: IData object containing the data to be converted to flat file format. The structure must match the structure defined in the flat file schema.

inputs.name: ffSchema
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the flat file schema that describes the structure of the output flat file.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Character encoding to use when writing the flat file data. If not specified, the encoding specified in the flat file schema is used. If no encoding is specified in the schema, the default system encoding is used.

inputs.name: validate
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether to validate the data against the flat file schema before conversion. Set to: true to validate the data. false to skip validation. This is the default.

outputs.name: string
outputs.description: String representation of the flat file data formatted according to the flat file schema.

inputs.name: ffValues
inputs.description: IData object containing the data to be converted to flat file format. The structure must match the structure defined in the flat file schema.

inputs.name: ffSchema
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the flat file schema that describes the structure of the output flat file.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Character encoding to use when writing the flat file data. If not specified, the encoding specified in the flat file schema is used. If no encoding is specified in the schema, the default system encoding is used.

inputs.name: validate
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether to validate the data against the flat file schema before conversion. Set to: true to validate the data. false to skip validation. This is the default.

outputs.name: bytes
outputs.description: Byte array representation of the flat file data formatted according to the flat file schema.

inputs.name: ffValues
inputs.description: IData object containing the data to be converted to flat file format. The structure must match the structure defined in the flat file schema.

inputs.name: ffSchema
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the flat file schema that describes the structure of the output flat file.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Character encoding to use when writing the flat file data. If not specified, the encoding specified in the flat file schema is used. If no encoding is specified in the schema, the default system encoding is used.

inputs.name: validate
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether to validate the data against the flat file schema before conversion. Set to: true to validate the data. false to skip validation. This is the default.

outputs.name: stream
outputs.description: InputStream containing the flat file data formatted according to the flat file schema.

inputs.name: ffSchema
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the flat file schema whose properties you want to retrieve.

outputs.name: schemaProperties
outputs.description: IData object containing the properties of the flat file schema, including format type (DELIMITED, FIXED_LENGTH, etc.), delimiters, record definitions, and field specifications.

inputs.name: ffData
inputs.description: Flat file data to be parsed. The data can be a String, byte array, InputStream, or java.io.Reader object.

inputs.name: ffSchema
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the flat file schema that describes the structure of the flat file data.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Character encoding to use when reading the flat file data. If not specified, the encoding specified in the flat file schema is used.

inputs.name: validate
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether to validate each record against the flat file schema. Set to: true to validate records. false to skip validation. This is the default.

outputs.name: ffIterator
outputs.description: Iterator object that can be used to process flat file records one at a time. Use pub.flatFile:getNextRecord to retrieve records from the iterator.

inputs.name: ffIterator
inputs.description: Iterator object created by pub.flatFile:createFFIterator.

outputs.name: ffValues
outputs.description: IData object containing the next parsed record from the flat file. Returns null when no more records are available.

outputs.name: hasMoreRecords
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether more records are available in the iterator. Set to: true if more records are available. false if no more records are available.

inputs.name: ffIterator
inputs.description: Iterator object to be closed. This releases any resources associated with the iterator.
